## [03:01 - 06:01]

Now what's even more important than that, can't stress this enough, is your headline especially in the context of the system that I'm going to show you. Here's why. You're going to be connecting with people and when you connect with them, you'll get a screen or they will get a screen that has you, your name, your headline and a custom message. They won't see the profile summary unless they click through. So think of this like a headline in an ad. It needs to grab them, prove to them to be relevant and hopefully trustworthy, flattering in a way to you. And you want it to kind of spark interest in them. This is very basic but very, very important. Okay, so headline crucial. The next most important thing is going to be your summary. Now, the first thing I want to do is point out a sort of stylistic opinion that I have which is that you should definitely not write about yourself in the third person. It doesn't feel very real. Everyone knows you wrote it in the first place. This isn't a bio which I've had to write some recently and it's no fun anyway. ## [06:01 - 09:02]

I do recommend that you do that. And I'll give you another example here of another profile. So here's Matt's profile. Matt is a fellow agency owner and you can see that he's taken a slightly different approach from what I do, who I help. And Matt is an expert in the space, as it were, having worked on campaigns for Heineken, FreshDirect, Tidal. Obviously, he's been around the block in this area. Now you can see he's taking a different approach. He's saying, he's talking about story. ## [09:02 - 11:54]

His banner image has video. He's got an easily recognizable, even the green screen background, I think makes a lot of sense for him to have that, right? And it stands out on the platform. So these are all things that Matt has done to make his profile stand out, make his summary relevant to people. And I think he's done a great job. So obviously he's a pretty credible guy and he went to a really good school too. And you don't need all of those elements in your profile to be successful, but I do recommend that you put your experience and hopefully your experience will show the kind of path that you came on to become the expert in your niche. But most people will never make it there. You're lucky if they read your whole profile.
